content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Commission
March 
■8′  ■99■
Lou Haddad
5 Deer Stalker Path
Monterey, cA 93940
Re: Your Request for Infornal Assistance
our File No: I -91-13 2
Dear Mr. Haddad:
This is in response to your question in connection with
disposition of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act (the
"Act"1.1 Because your question requests general guidance, this
letter is in the nature of informal assistance.z
OUESTION
May a defeated candj.date transfer funds remaining in his or
her campaign bank account to the carnpaign account of a ballot
measure cornmittee controlled by the candidate if the transfer
takes place before the end of the postelection reporting period?
CONCLUSION
Prior to the end of the postelection reporting period, a
candidate nay transfer funds rernaining in his or her candidate
account to the carnpaign account of a ba11ot measure controlled by
the candidate.
1 Gor".rr."nt code sections 81ooo-91o15. All statutory references
are to the covernnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 cal-ifornia code of Regulations section
18ooo, et seq. Al-1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regrulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Governnent Code Section 83114r' 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section L8329 (c) (3) . )
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804つ807 0 (916)322″ 5660


FACTS
You recently ran for public office and were defeated. Your
carnpaign corumittee, the Haddad for Council Connittee, had funds
remaining in its campaign account subsequent to the eLection.
Prior to the end of the reporting period following the election,
you transferred the renaj-ning funds to another committee which you
control , the t{ontereyians for open Government connittee, which was
forned to qual,ify an initiative 
for the ba1lot and to support the
initiative 
after its qualification.
You are writing to confirm that the transfer of funds betr.reen
comrnittees is proper under the Act.
ANALYSI S
The Fair PoIitica1 Practices Cornmission is not pernitted to
gj-ve advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329, copy
enclosed.) Hovrever, we can advise you generally regarding the
provisions of the Act that relate to transfers of campaign funds.
A recent Federal Court decision, Service Emplovees
International Union. et a1. v. Fair Political, Practices
Commission, 747 F.Supp 580 (E.D. calif. 
1990) (nSEfUrr), held that
the transfer ban contained i,n Section 85304 is unconstitutional to
the extent that its purpose is to enforce fiscal year contribution
lirnits.3 
Therefore, as a general ru1e, candidates may now
transfer funds freely among their own committees and may also
transfer funds to other candidates' committees. (See Gunter Advice
Letter, No. I-90-61"6. ) Ho$rever, restrictions still 
apply with
respect to special elections and with respect to local- eLectj.ons
subject to valid Iocal lirnits. 
(see Spraque Advice Letter, No. I-
9L-029 and Riffenberq Advice Letter, No. A-90-761, copies
enclosed. )
Funds remaining in a canpaign account after an el-ected
officer leaves office or at the end of the oostelection reportinc,
period followinq the defeat of a candidate become itsurplus funds,rr
which are subje-t to the restrictions of Section 89519. section
89519 provides as follos/s:
Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of
the postelection reporting period following the
defeat of a candidate for elective office,
$/hichever occurs 1ast, campaign funds raised after
January L, 1989t under the controL of the former
candidlte or elected officer shall be considered
surplus carnpaign funds and shall- be disclosed
purluant to Chipter 4 ( commencing with Section
ilcrool and shall be used only for the following
purposes :
The deC■S■On ■n SEIU iS Currently on appeal.


(a) The payment of outstanding canpaign debts
or elected officer,s expenses.
(b) The pro rata repayment of contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious or similar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, where no
substantial part of the proceeds will have a
material financial effect on the former candidate
or elected officer, any member of his or her
irnmediate fanily, or his or her campaj-gn treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a political party or
comrnittee so Long as the funds are not used to make
contributions in support of or opposition to a
candidate for elective office.
(e) contributions to support or oppose any
candidate for federal office, any candidate for
elective office in a state other than california,
or any ballot neasure.
Thus, a defeated candidate who waits until after the end of
the postelection reporting period to dispose of funds remaining in
his or her campaign account is linited to the provisions of
Section 89519 in deterrnining how to utilize his or her surplus
funds. Defeated candidates lrho do not wait until after the end of
the postelection reporting period to di.spose of their funds are
not subject to the strictures of Section 89519.
I trust the above ansr{ers your questions. If you feel you
have need for further information, please do not hesitate to
contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
sincerely,
SCOTT HALLABRIN
SUSAN L. BOBROW
counsel′  Legal DiviSion
SH:SLB
Enc■osures
